title: Object storage system with meta object replication
abstract: Example object storage systems, meta object generators, and methods provide meta objects for replication of configuration data between data object stores. A meta object may be generated that includes configuration data. A meta object identifier may be assigned to the meta object. The meta object may be stored in a first object store with related data objects and may be replicated to a second object data store with the related data objects.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A system, comprising: a first obj ect data store; a second object data store; a metadata store associated with the first object data store and configured to store configuration data for the first object data store; and a meta object generator configured to: generate a meta object including meta object data selected from the configuration data; assign a meta object identifier to the meta object; and store the meta object with related data objects, wherein a plurality of data objects replicated to the second object data store include the meta object and the related data objects. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein: the first object data store is configured to store the plurality of data objects in a data object bucket; the meta object data includes bucket configuration data; and the related data objects include data objects stored in the data object bucket. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the meta object identifier includes: a globally unique identifier for the meta object; and a meta tag appended to a key value associated with the globally unique identifier. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein: the meta object generator is further configured to generate a hidden header value; the meta object identifier includes the hidden header value; and the first object data store is further configured to require the hidden header value to access the meta obj ect. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein the meta object generator is further configured to encrypt the meta object data in the meta object. 6. The system of claim 1, further comprising: a replication engine configured to replicate the plurality of data objects from the first object data store to the second object data store. 7. The system of claim 1, further comprising: a meta object update handler configured to: detect changed configuration data in the configuration data; and initiate the meta object generator to generate an updated meta object including the changed configuration data. 8. The system of claim 7, wherein the meta object update handler is further configured to periodically initiate the meta object generator at predetermined intervals. 9. The system of claim 1, further comprising: a disaster recovery manager configured to: recover the plurality of data obj ects from the second obj ect data store to a recovery obj ect data store; and rebuild, based on the configuration data in the meta object, configuration data in a recovery metadata store. 10. A computer-implemented method, comprising: generating a meta object, wherein: the meta object includes meta object data selected from configuration data; and the configuration data configures a first object data store; assigning a meta object identifier to the meta object; storing the meta object with related data objects in the first object data store; and replicating a plurality of data objects to a second object data store, wherein the plurality of data objects includes the meta object and the related data objects. 11. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, further comprising: configuring the first object data store to store the plurality of data objects in a data object bucket, wherein: the meta object data is bucket configuration data; and the related data objects are data objects stored in the data object bucket. 12. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, wherein the meta object identifier includes: a globally unique identifier for the meta object; and a meta tag appended to a key value associated with the globally unique identifier. 13. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, further comprising: generating a hidden header value, wherein the meta object identifier includes the hidden header value; and accessing, using the hidden header value, the meta object, wherein the first object data store is configured to require the hidden header value to access the meta object. 14. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, further comprising: encrypting the meta object data in the meta object. 15. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, further comprising: selecting the configuration data for the meta object data from a metadata store associated with the first object data store. 16. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, further comprising: detecting changed configuration data in the configuration data; and generating an updated meta object including the changed configuration data. 17. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, further comprising: selecting a predetermined interval for meta object updates; and periodically generating, at the predetermined interval, updated meta objects including changed configuration data. 18. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, further comprising: recovering the plurality of data objects from the second object data store to a recovery object data store; and rebuilding, based on the configuration data in the meta object, configuration data in a recovery metadata store. 19. A system, comprising: a first obj ect data store; a metadata store associated with the first object data store and configured to store configuration data for the first object data store; means for generating a meta object including meta object data selected from the configuration data; means for assigning a meta object identifier to the meta object; means for storing the meta object with related data objects in the first object data store; and 

 means for replicating a plurality of data objects to a second object data store, wherein the plurality of data objects replicated to the second object data store include the meta object and the related data objects. 20. The system of claim 19, further comprising: means for configuring the first object data store to store the plurality of data objects in a data object bucket, wherein: the meta object data is bucket configuration data; and the related data objects are data objects stored in the data object bucket.
